title: Bumble (BMBL) Stock Forecast: Analyst Ratings, Predictions & Price Target
text: Bumble has been analyzed by multiple analysts with varying sentiment on the stock. The consensus rating of Hold has 85% suggesting holding the stock and 15% advising selling, with 0% recommending a buy. Based on analysis of 27 Wall Street analysts, BMBL has a neutral consensus with a median price target of $5.00, ranging from $3.50 to $7.00. Following Q1 2026 results, analysts at Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on the stock and lowered their price target to $4 from $5, citing ongoing user declines and a weaker near-term revenue trajectory. Bumble forecast second-quarter revenue in the range of $205 million to $213 million, below analyst expectations of around $215 million. The company also guided for adjusted EBITDA of $65 million to $70 million for the period. Bumble is experiencing weaker net paying user additions, resulting in slowing revenue growth and diminishing opportunities for average revenue per user (ARPU) expansion. The introduction of Bee, an AI assistant, is expected to enhance onboarding and recommendations, with early tests showing that members are more willing to share context, improving match quality.
